summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering the 2024 fiscal year true-up, which showed overall unencumbered fund growth of $500,000 to $5.1 million. The board approved the purchase of a new Bobcat skid steer for the service department, a Chevrolet Tahoe for the fire department, declared surplus police items for sale, amended the Station 18 construction budget, and hired one full-time and six part-time firefighter/EMTs after an executive session.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for January 15-28, 2025
Declared surplus police items (ammunition, magazines, 2014 Ford Fusion) for sale on GovDeals
Amended Station 18 demolition and rebuild budget (Fund 4901) for 2025 to $8,720,260.84
Increased appropriations in motor vehicles fund by $74,167.39 for Bobcat skid steer purchase
Approved purchase of 2024 T-66 Bobcat compact tracked loader skid steer with accessories for $74,167.39 (Trustee Hodges abstained)
Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter EMT Austin Taylor effective 1/31/25
Approved purchase of 2024 Chevrolet Tahoe from McCluskey Chevrolet at fleet price of $54,445
Certified township road mileage at 47.1 miles as of 12/31/2024
Entered executive session at 6:51 p.m. per ORC 121.22 G1
Credited part-time firefighter EMT Rick Holland with 119 hours of PTO
Hired full-time firefighter paramedic Brandon Kilgore at step one with one-year probation
Hired part-time firefighter EMT Jason Stevens at $17.50/hr effective 2/9/2025
Hired part-time firefighter EMT Tyler Pilcher at $17.50/hr effective 2/9/2025
Hired part-time firefighter EMT Tristan Smiley at $17.50/hr effective 2/9/2025
Hired part-time firefighter EMT James Jansen at $17.50/hr effective 2/9/2025
Hired part-time firefighter EMT Damian Blum at $17.50/hr effective 2/9/2025
Hired part-time firefighter EMT Thomas Jetter at $17.50/hr effective 2/9/2025
action_items: Bob Rose to meet individually with trustees to finalize Salute to Leaders selection before the deadline
Chad Meadows to research Bobcat front loader options (new and slightly used) with pricing, extended warranty, and Sourcewell discount through rep Bernie
Chad Meadows to explore selling the 2017 Case 580 backhoe on GovDeals vs. trade-in and bring dollar amounts for both options back to the board
Staff to verify lighting district reassessment took effect by comparing first 2025 auditor check to prior year
Chief Myers to price upfitting costs for the new Chevrolet Tahoe
Chief Rose to process surplus items for sale on GovDeals
key_amounts: $342,000 total reimbursement checks to entities that assisted with 2022 tornado recovery (75% from Ohio EMA, 25% township)
$122,000 decline in general fund due to tornado reimbursement expense
$350,000 increase in police unencumbered funds in 2024
$50,000 grant for body-worn cameras offsetting planned expense
$917,000 ending unencumbered balance for service department funds
$5.1 million total ending unencumbered balance (excluding special funds)
$12.4 million actual total revenue for 2024
$9.7 million actual total expenses for 2024
$8,720,260.84 revised Station 18 demolition and rebuild budget
$7.4 million Conger construction contract for Station 18
$1.1 million contracted services for Station 18 (architect, exhaust, generator, etc.)
$108,000 for Station 18 furnishings and operational items
$74,167.39 for 2024 T-66 Bobcat skid steer with accessories
$54,445 fleet price for 2024 Chevrolet Tahoe
$55,000 accumulated in opioid settlement fund
$44,000 trade-in value offered for 2017 Case 580 backhoe
$8,000 spent on backhoe repairs in last year and a half
$361,000 ARPA funds spent down to zero in 2024
$17.50/hour pay rate for part-time firefighter EMTs
$1,500 saved on Bobcat through Sourcewell membership
notable_topics: Electrical aggregation inquiry - positive feedback from other Clermont County communities
2024 fiscal year true-up across all township funds
Station 18 fire station demolition and rebuild progress - Conger to deliver trailer week of Feb 24, foundation drawings expected March 6
Surplus police equipment and vehicle disposal
Service department equipment: new Bobcat skid steer purchase and future backhoe-to-front-loader replacement discussion
Fire department Chevrolet Tahoe purchase for staff vehicle replacement
Salute to Leaders nomination discussion - Kevin Maloff for Homearama and Clermont County Land Bank mentioned
Hiring of one full-time and six part-time firefighter/EMTs
Resignation of part-time firefighter EMT Austin Taylor to Anderson Township
2022 tornado reimbursement to assisting communities completed
ARPA funds fully expended by end of 2024
Government Finance Officers Association unencumbered fund recommendations (16.7% minimum vs. township's 54.5%)
Ohio Township Association Conference upcoming in Columbus
notes: Trustee Hodges abstained from the Bobcat skid steer purchase vote without stating a specific reason (began to say 'Due to my...'). The backup meta unit was returned from City of Milford. Station 18 construction is being funded entirely without local tax dollars (insurance, Ohio EMA, and state funds). The board discussed at length the merits of switching from a backhoe to a front rubber tire loader for salt loading operations due to salt corrosion issues. Executive session lasted approximately 51 minutes (6:51 to 7:42 p.m.). The meeting referenced the upcoming Ohio Township Association Conference in Columbus.
title: BOT 1-28-25
